description: Side-anchored modal panel. Despite the distinct name, Sheet is built on Radix Dialog; it is the same primitive as Dialog, with variants that slide in from the top, bottom, left, or right. Focus trapping, aria-labelledby / aria-describedby, and Escape-to-close behavior are all identical to Dialog.

keyboard.action: Cycles focus through the sheet's focusable elements.
Cycles focus backwards within the sheet.
Activates the focused button / submits the focused form.

focus.notes: Focus is trapped inside the sheet while open and restored to the triggering element on close. A built-in close button with sr-only "Close" label is rendered in the top-right corner.

announcements: Announced with its role, SheetTitle, and SheetDescription in reading order.
The close button announces "Close" via its sr-only label.
screenReader.notes: Always render a SheetTitle (use VisuallyHidden if you don't want it on screen); Radix Dialog warns otherwise and screen readers announce an unnamed dialog.

contrast.text: Title uses foreground, description uses text-muted-foreground. Both meet WCAG AA on bg-background
contrast.ui: Overlay uses bg-black/80; border on the entering side uses border token. Meets AA non-text contrast

motion.reducedMotion: Sheets slide in via data-[state=open]:slide-in-from-* utilities with 300–500ms durations. Gate with motion-reduce:animate-none if strict compliance matters; the result is a plain fade.

consumerResponsibilities: Always render SheetTitle; use VisuallyHidden when you don't want it visible.
Pick a side that doesn't cover the primary page content unless full-width is intended; bottom sheets work well on mobile.
For non-modal side panels that should let users interact with the page, use a custom layout rather than Sheet.
